title: Rafa at the Airport
description: Airport and travel vocabulary. Boarding pass, luggage, security, delayed, cancelled. Plus the essentials of check-in English.
summary_pt_br: Vocabulário de aeroporto + viagem: check-in, boarding pass, luggage, gate, delayed, cancelled. Diálogos típicos no balcão. Armadilha: 'luggage' é INCONTÁVEL — diga 'two pieces of luggage', nunca 'two luggages'.

scenes.text_en:
Rafa checked in two hours early.
He had one CARRY-ON bag and one LUGGAGE.
His flight was DELAYED by thirty minutes.
The GATE changed twice. He ran. He made it.
The BOARDING PASS on his phone beeped at security.
scenes.text_pt:
O Rafa fez o check-in duas horas antes.
Levou uma bagagem DE MÃO e uma DESPACHADA.
O voo ATRASOU trinta minutos.
O PORTÃO mudou duas vezes. Correu. Conseguiu.
O CARTÃO DE EMBARQUE no celular apitou na segurança.

scenes.title: Luggage is uncountable
scenes.body_en: In English, LUGGAGE and BAGGAGE are uncountable. Never 'two luggages'. Say 'two pieces of luggage' or 'two bags'. Same rule: INFORMATION, ADVICE, MONEY — all uncountable. Brazilians often try to pluralize — don't.
scenes.body_pt: LUGGAGE / BAGGAGE são INCONTÁVEIS em inglês. NUNCA 'two luggages'. Diga 'two pieces of luggage' ou 'two bags'. Mesma regra para information, advice, money. Não pluralize.

examples.en: I have a lot of luggage. NOT 'a lot of luggages'.

turns.en: Can I see your passport, please?
Here you go. Window seat, please.
Just one bag. My carry-on stays with me.
turns.pt: Só uma mala. A de mão fica comigo.

exercise.question: Which is correct?
options: I have two pieces of luggage
exercise.explanation: 'Luggage' is uncountable. 'Two pieces of luggage' or 'two bags' — never 'two luggages'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Luggage é incontável. 'Two pieces of luggage'.

exercise.question: Complete: 'My flight is ___ by two hours.' (not on time)
Complete: "I am flying to ___."
exercise.explanation: The full phrase is: "I am flying to Orlando."

exercise.question: Pick the best response. ('Sim, aqui está.')
exercise.explanation: The correct response is: "Yes, here it is."

scenes.title: At the check-in counter
scenes.pt_summary: Check-in em aeroporto — bags, window/aisle, boarding pass.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Good morning. Where are you flying today?
turns.pt: Bom dia. Pra onde voa hoje?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: May I see your passport, please?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How many bags are you checking in?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Here is your boarding pass. Gate twelve.
turns.pt: Aqui está seu cartão de embarque. Portão doze.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Thank you. What time is boarding?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.pt: O embarque começa às dez e quarenta e cinco.

scenes.title: Listening story — Rafa at the Airport
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Airline agent and a student. Airport and travel vocabulary. Boarding pass, luggage, security, delayed, cancelled. Plus the essentials of check-in English. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Airline agent
paragraphs.en: Good morning. Where are you flying today?

paragraphs.speaker: Airline agent
paragraphs.en: May I see your passport, please?

paragraphs.speaker: Airline agent
paragraphs.en: How many bags are you checking in?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Airline agent. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Rafa at the Airport". Airport and travel vocabulary. Boarding pass, luggage, security, delayed, cancelled. Plus the essentials of check-in English. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Rafa at the Airport". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Rafa at the Airport'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Rafa at the Airport".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: Perfect English Grammar
sources.pt_hint: Regras de gramática + exercícios interativos gratuitos.

sources.label: British Council · A2
sources.pt_hint: Leituras A2 com exercícios de compreensão.
